risks: Cache storage account (stasrcacheeus001) uses Standard_LRS; Microsoft recommends ZRS for zone-redundant replication staging — a zone failure in East US could interrupt replication data flow
DNS failback Function App (func-dns-failback-eus) is deployed only in East US; if that region suffers an outage the automated DNS failback orchestration will be unavailable

No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Application Insights, Log Analytics) are included — ASR replication health, Function App invocations, and Traffic Manager probe results will lack centralized observability
No network isolation controls (private endpoints, NSGs, or service endpoints) are defined for the storage account or Recovery Services vault, leaving management and data planes publicly accessible
No Recovery Plan resource (Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationRecoveryPlans) is included to orchestrate sequenced multi-VM failover and failback
Traffic Manager profile has no explicit endpoint child resources defined; East and West endpoints must be provisioned for priority-based failover to function
correctionsRecommended: Upgrade cache storage SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS to ensure replication staging survives an availability zone failure
Deploy a second DNS failback Function App in West US (or relocate to West US) so automation is available when the primary region is down
Add an Application Insights resource and Log Analytics workspace to capture ASR replication telemetry, Function App logs, and Traffic Manager health probe metrics
Add a dedicated storage account for the Function App runtime (Azure Functions requires its own storage account separate from the ASR cache account)
Add a Recovery Plan resource under the vault to define VM failover groups, sequencing, and custom script actions
Add explicit Traffic Manager endpoint resources for East US (priority 1) and West US (priority 2)
Consider adding private endpoints for the cache storage account and Recovery Services vault to restrict network access
Add RBAC role assignments granting the Function App managed identity DNS Zone Contributor on the DNS zone and Reader on the Traffic Manager profile
hardDependencies: Function App (func-dns-failback-eus) requires a dedicated backing storage account for the Azure Functions runtime; this storage account is absent from the plan and the Function App will fail to deploy without it
